title: L2MAC: Large Language Model Automatic Computer for Extensive Code Generation // van der Schaar Lab
summary: This source introduces L2MAC, a framework designed to overcome the context window limitations of Large Language Models (LLMs) for generating extensive and consistent outputs, such as large codebases. It implements a practical stored-program automatic computer (von Neumann architecture) where a Control Unit manages external memory read/write operations, dynamically manages the LLM's context based on sequential instructions, and iteratively corrects errors. The instantiation Code-L2MAC excels at generating functional, error-minimal codebases with user-specified features and achieved a 90.2% Pass@1 score on the HumanEval benchmark. The framework is accepted for presentation at ICLR 2024 and the corresponding code is publicly available.
author: Joseph Ginsberg, Sam Holt (van der Schaar Lab)
